summary: Environment-gated decrypt-and-eval payload targeting AI coding agent sandboxes
details: The package advertises itself as TypeScript form-validation utilities but ships only a few trivial regex validators alongside a hidden dropper. On require() (or any CLI invocation), index.js evaluates a gate that checks the machine's hostname for substrings 'devbox', 'ubuntu-fc-uvm', or 'fc-uvm', checks for the existence of /app/.git, and checks whether the JULES_SESSION_ID environment variable is set (the fingerprint of the Google Jules coding agent sandbox). If any condition matches, it reads the shipped opaque blob lib/.perf.dat, AES-256-CBC decrypts it with a hardcoded key/IV, and executes the resulting JavaScript via `new Function(_r)()` (index.js:34-43). To evade static scanners, module names are assembled via string concatenation ('f'+'s', 'crypt'+'o', 'pat'+'h', 'aes-256-cb'+'c') and failures are silently swallowed. None of this is required for form validation. The hostname/env gating ensures developer workstations stay dormant while the payload fires inside agentic CI/sandbox environments — turning any AI agent that installs this dependency into an execution vector for attacker-controlled code whose contents cannot be audited from the source.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
